content:
# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Overview

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

When to use:
- Need existing login/session
- MFA makes fresh browser login hard
- Need inspectable live browser
- Admin console automation

Inputs required:
- Browser launched with remote debugging
- Local port
- User data directory/profile
- Target URL

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.

# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Execution Steps

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Start Chrome/Edge with remote debugging bound to localhost and a dedicated user-data-dir/profile.
- Check http://127.0.0.1:{port}/json/version or /json/list.
- Connect Playwright or CDP client to the browser WebSocket endpoint.
- Reuse logged-in state but verify account/profile before acting.
- Close debug browser or port when done.

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.

# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Commands or APIs

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- --remote-debugging-port=9222
- --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1
- http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/version
- chromium.connectOverCDP(...)

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.

# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Verification

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- /json/version returns browser info
- Automation sees target page
- Logged-in account is verified
- Final screenshot proves outcome

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.

# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Failure Modes

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Port not listening
- Wrong profile/user data dir
- Chrome blocks default profile use
- Security exposure
- Account mismatch

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.

# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Safety Notes

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Bind debugging port to 127.0.0.1 only.
- Do not expose CDP port to network.
- Confirm account before irreversible actions.
- Close debug session after task.

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.

# chrome-cdp-existing-browser-session - Handoff Report

Automation type: browser_cdp
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP, Playwright
Goal: Connect automation to an existing logged-in Chromium browser through a local remote debugging port.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Port/profile
- Account verified
- Pages/tabs used
- Screenshots
- Any irreversible action skipped or confirmed

AI summary: Use CDP when an agent must control an already logged-in Chrome/Edge session: launch with localhost remote-debugging port, verify /json/version, connect, confirm account, and close the port after use.
